essence: Maintains and repairs the rail tracks and infrastructure that keep trains running safely and reliably.

identity.title: Railway Track Maintenance Operative
identity.summary: Inspects, repairs, and maintains railway tracks, sleepers, ballast, and lineside infrastructure to ensure safe train operations.

salary.notes: Night‑shift teams, possession work, and safety‑critical roles can exceed £50k-£60k+.

qualifications: Knowledge of track components and maintenance techniques
Understanding of safety procedures around live railways
Physical fitness and ability to work outdoors in all conditions

lifestyle.description: Railway track maintenance operatives work on rail lines inspecting, repairing, and maintaining track, sleepers, ballast, and drainage. The role often involves night shifts, weekend work, and operating during engineering possessions when trains are not running. It is physical, safety‑critical, and essential to the reliability of the rail network. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy teamwork, outdoor work, and contributing to public transport safety.

habits: Checks tools and PPE before each shift
Communicates clearly with the engineering team
Keeps work areas controlled and hazard‑free
Maintains accurate inspection and repair records
